questions.passage: Sleep deprivation has long been studied in relation to cognitive performance, but recent research highlights its equally serious consequences for emotional regulation. When people go without adequate sleep, the amygdala — the brain region associated with emotional processing — becomes significantly more reactive to negative stimuli. At the same time, the prefrontal cortex, which normally moderates emotional responses, shows reduced connectivity with the amygdala. The result is that sleep-deprived individuals tend to experience stronger negative emotions and have less capacity to regulate them. This imbalance manifests not only in irritability and mood swings but also in heightened anxiety and a reduced ability to empathize with others. Crucially, these emotional effects occur even at levels of sleep restriction that individuals themselves do not perceive as impairing — people often feel they have adapted to getting six hours of sleep when, neurologically, they have not. This makes sleep-related emotional dysregulation a particularly difficult problem to identify and address.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: impact of sleep loss on emotional regulation and reactivity
cognitive benefits of establishing a consistent sleep schedule
relationship between anxiety disorders and chronic insomnia
role of the prefrontal cortex in long-term memory formation
methods for improving emotional empathy through sleep therapy

explanations.correct: ① 수면 부족이 정서 조절과 반응성에 미치는 영향을 직접 포착합니다. 지문은 "the amygdala becomes significantly more reactive to negative stimuli"(편도체가 부정적 자극에 훨씬 더 반응적으로 변한다)와 "the prefrontal cortex shows reduced connectivity"(전전두피질의 연결성이 감소한다)를 제시하며, 결과적으로 "sleep-deprived individuals tend to experience stronger negative emotions and have less capacity to regulate them"(수면 부족 상태에서는 더 강한 부정적 감정을 경험하면서 이를 조절하는 능력은 떨어진다)고 명시합니다.

wrong.2: ② 수면 스케줄의 인지적 이점을 다루나, 지문은 스케줄이 아닌 수면 부족의 신경학적 결과에 초점을 맞추므로 범위를 벗어납니다.
wrong.3: ③ 불안이 증상 중 하나로 언급되지만, 지문의 주제는 수면 부족으로 인한 더 넓은 정서적 조절 장애이지 불안 장애 자체가 아닙니다.
wrong.4: ④ 기억 형성은 지문에서 전혀 언급되지 않으며, 전전두피질은 오직 감정 조절 역할로만 논의됩니다.
wrong.5: ⑤ 수면 치료법은 전혀 언급되지 않습니다. 지문은 설명적이지, 처방적이지 않습니다.

explanations.key_point: 수면 부족 → 편도체 과활성 + 전전두피질 연결성 감소 → 부정적 감정 증폭 & 조절 능력 저하.

self_check.passage_topic: sleep deprivation impairing emotional regulation via amygdala-prefrontal imbalance
self_check.main_argument: Sleep deprivation disrupts the amygdala-prefrontal balance, intensifying negative emotions and reducing regulatory capacity — often unnoticed by the individual.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 1 directly captures the central claim: sleep loss affects both emotional reactivity and the ability to regulate it.

distractor_analysis.choice_2: Sleep scheduling is not discussed; the passage focuses on the neurological consequences of deprivation, not schedule benefits.
distractor_analysis.choice_3: Anxiety is one symptom mentioned, but the passage's subject is broader emotional dysregulation caused by sleep loss, not anxiety disorders per se.
distractor_analysis.choice_4: Memory formation is never mentioned; the prefrontal cortex is discussed only in its emotional moderating role.
distractor_analysis.choice_5: Sleep therapy is not discussed; the passage is explanatory, not prescriptive.

self_check.difficulty: easy — the passage clearly and directly states its thesis; correct answer paraphrases the main point straightforwardly.

questions.passage: Across many animal species, color plays a fundamental role in communication between individuals. In some cases, vivid coloration serves as a warning signal — a phenomenon known as aposematism — advertising to potential predators that the animal is toxic or unpalatable. The poison dart frog's brilliant red-and-blue patterning, for example, communicates danger so effectively that predators with prior exposure learn to avoid similarly colored prey. In other species, color functions as camouflage, allowing animals to blend into their environment and evade detection. The Arctic fox shifts from brown in summer to white in winter, tracking seasonal changes in background color. Perhaps most fascinatingly, some animals use color dynamically and within milliseconds — the cuttlefish can ripple complex patterns of color across its skin to confuse predators, attract mates, or communicate with rivals. Whether static or dynamic, animal coloration consistently serves as a channel through which critical survival and reproductive information is transmitted.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: diverse communicative functions of color in the animal kingdom
evolutionary history of warning coloration in poisonous species
limitations of camouflage strategies in modern environments
mechanisms by which predators learn to identify toxic prey
comparison of static and dynamic color-change abilities in animals

explanations.correct: ① 동물계에서 색의 다양한 의사소통 기능을 정확히 포착합니다. 지문은 경고색(독화살 개구리), 위장(북극여우), 동적 신호(갑오징어) 등 여러 기능을 나열한 뒤 "animal coloration consistently serves as a channel through which critical survival and reproductive information is transmitted"(동물의 체색은 생존·번식 정보를 전달하는 채널로 일관되게 기능한다)고 결론짓습니다.

wrong.2: ② 진화적 역사는 전혀 언급되지 않으며, 경고색은 여러 기능 중 하나일 뿐입니다.
wrong.3: ③ 위장 전략의 한계는 언급되지 않습니다. 지문은 위장을 효과적인 것으로 제시합니다.
wrong.4: ④ 포식자 학습은 한 메커니즘으로 간략히 언급될 뿐, 중심 주제가 아닙니다.
wrong.5: ⑤ 정적/동적 색 변화 비교는 마지막 문장에서 잠깐 언급되지만 지문의 주된 구성 원리가 아닙니다.

explanations.key_point: 동물의 체색은 경고·위장·동적 신호 등 다양한 기능을 통해 생존·번식 정보를 전달하는 의사소통 채널이다.

self_check.passage_topic: animal coloration as multi-functional communication channel
self_check.main_argument: Color in animals serves diverse communicative purposes — warning, camouflage, and dynamic signaling — all ultimately transmitting survival or reproductive information.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 1 captures the breadth of the passage's argument: multiple functions of color across different species.

distractor_analysis.choice_2: Too narrow; evolutionary history is not discussed, and warning coloration is only one of several functions examined.
distractor_analysis.choice_3: Limitations of camouflage are never mentioned; the passage presents camouflage as effective.
distractor_analysis.choice_4: Predator learning is mentioned briefly as one mechanism, not the central focus.

self_check.difficulty: easy — the passage is clearly organized around multiple functions; the correct answer uses the word 'diverse' which matches the passage's structure.

questions.passage: In competitive markets, first-mover advantage is often cited as a powerful source of sustained competitive advantage. The logic is intuitive: the first firm to enter a market can establish brand loyalty, lock in key distribution channels, and set industry standards before rivals appear. However, the empirical record reveals a more complicated picture. Many of history's most successful companies were not pioneers but fast followers — firms that entered after the initial innovator had demonstrated demand, revealed product flaws, and educated consumers. Microsoft, for instance, rarely invented the technologies it came to dominate; it refined and marketed them more effectively than their originators. The advantage of the fast follower lies in being able to improve upon an existing concept without bearing the heavy costs of market creation, technical failure, and consumer education that burden pioneers. Being second, it turns out, can be strategically superior to being first — provided the follower is fast and capable enough to capitalize on the pioneer's groundwork.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: strategic advantages of entering a market after the initial innovator
reasons why brand loyalty is difficult to sustain in competitive markets
importance of timing product launches to align with consumer demand
ways that technology companies protect their first-mover advantages
relationship between innovation investment and long-term market dominance

explanations.correct: ① 시장 진입 후발 주자의 전략적 이점을 정확히 포착합니다. 지문은 "Being second, it turns out, can be strategically superior to being first"(두 번째가 첫 번째보다 전략적으로 우월할 수 있다)고 명시하며, 선발 주자가 소비자 교육·기술 실패·시장 개척 비용을 부담하는 반면 후발 주자는 이를 활용할 수 있다고 논증합니다.

wrong.2: ② 브랜드 충성도 유지의 어려움은 논의되지 않습니다. 브랜드 충성도는 선발 주자의 이점으로 잠깐 언급될 뿐입니다.
wrong.3: ③ 제품 출시 타이밍과 소비자 수요 조율은 지문의 핵심이 아닙니다.
wrong.4: ④ 선발 주자 이점 보호 방법은 전혀 언급되지 않으며, 오히려 선발 주자가 지는 경우가 많다고 논증합니다.
wrong.5: ⑤ 혁신 투자와 시장 지배의 관계는 다루지 않습니다. 지문은 R&D 지출이 아닌 진입 순서에 초점을 맞춥니다.

explanations.key_point: 선발 주자 우위 통념과 달리, 빠른 후발 주자는 선발 주자의 비용·실패를 피하면서 이점을 활용할 수 있어 전략적으로 우월하다.

self_check.passage_topic: fast-follower advantage over first-mover in competitive markets
self_check.main_argument: Despite the common assumption that market pioneers win, fast followers often outperform them by capitalizing on groundwork laid without bearing pioneer costs.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 1 precisely captures the passage's counter-intuitive thesis: entering after the pioneer carries strategic advantages.

distractor_analysis.choice_2: Brand loyalty is mentioned as a pioneer advantage, not as a challenge for sustaining it.
distractor_analysis.choice_3: Timing is relevant but the passage focuses on the pioneer vs. follower distinction, not timing relative to consumer demand specifically.
distractor_analysis.choice_4: Protecting first-mover advantages is never discussed; the passage argues the opposite — pioneers often lose.
distractor_analysis.choice_5: Innovation investment is not the focus; the passage is about the strategic position of follower vs. pioneer, not R&D spending.

self_check.difficulty: standard — requires recognizing the counter-intuitive argument against conventional first-mover wisdom.

questions.passage: Modern cities generate enormous quantities of data — from traffic sensors and energy meters to mobile devices and surveillance cameras. Urban planners and policymakers have increasingly embraced the concept of the "smart city," in which these data streams are aggregated and analyzed in real time to optimize infrastructure, reduce energy consumption, and improve public services. Proponents argue that data-driven urban management can dramatically improve quality of life, reducing commute times, predicting infrastructure failures, and allocating emergency services more efficiently. Critics, however, raise concerns about the concentration of surveillance power and the risks of algorithmic governance. When decisions about policing, social services, or housing are delegated to automated systems trained on historical data, those systems can embed and amplify existing social inequalities. The smart city, its critics warn, risks becoming a platform for discriminatory management rather than equitable governance, as the people most affected by these systems often have the least ability to challenge or contest algorithmic decisions.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: potential and social risks of data-driven governance in smart cities
technical challenges of integrating diverse data systems in urban areas
economic benefits of reducing infrastructure costs through smart technology
role of citizen participation in designing urban data collection policies
comparison of smart city models adopted in different countries

explanations.correct: ① 스마트 시티의 잠재력과 사회적 위험을 모두 포착합니다. 지문은 데이터 기반 도시 관리가 "dramatically improve quality of life"(삶의 질을 극적으로 개선)할 수 있다는 긍정적 측면을 먼저 제시한 뒤, "algorithmic governance... can embed and amplify existing social inequalities"(알고리즘 거버넌스가 기존 사회 불평등을 내재화·증폭할 수 있다)는 비판을 통해 두 측면을 균형 있게 논의합니다.

wrong.2: ② 다양한 데이터 시스템 통합의 기술적 도전은 전혀 언급되지 않습니다. 지문은 공학이 아닌 거버넌스 결과에 초점을 맞춥니다.
wrong.3: ③ 인프라 비용 절감은 여러 혜택 중 하나로만 언급되며, 지문은 반론도 함께 제시합니다.
wrong.4: ④ 시민 참여는 논의되지 않습니다. 오히려 시민이 알고리즘 결정에 이의를 제기하기 어렵다고 언급합니다.
wrong.5: ⑤ 국가별 비교는 이루어지지 않습니다.

explanations.key_point: 스마트 시티는 효율성 혜택과 함께 알고리즘적 사회 불평등 강화라는 위험을 동시에 안고 있다.

self_check.passage_topic: smart city benefits and risks of algorithmic discrimination
self_check.main_argument: Smart city technologies offer real efficiency benefits but also pose risks of surveillance concentration and algorithmic reinforcement of social inequalities.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 1 captures both dimensions the passage explores: the potential (benefits) and social risks (discriminatory governance) of data-driven cities.

distractor_analysis.choice_2: Technical integration challenges are never mentioned; the discussion is about governance consequences, not engineering.

distractor_analysis.choice_4: Citizen participation is not discussed; the passage actually notes citizens have little ability to challenge algorithmic decisions.

questions.passage: The concept of narrative identity holds that individuals construct a coherent sense of self by integrating their experiences into an ongoing story — one with a protagonist, a past, and an imagined future. This story is not merely a neutral record of events but a selective and interpretive enterprise: people emphasize experiences that confirm their self-concept and revise or minimize those that contradict it. Psychologist Dan McAdams argues that this life story is the primary means by which adults give their lives meaning and purpose. Crucially, the stories people tell about themselves are not purely personal; they draw heavily on culturally available narrative templates — the hero's journey, the redemption arc, the rags-to-riches story — which provide ready-made structures for organizing experience. In this sense, individual identity is never entirely individual: it is co-authored by the cultural repertoire of stories a person has absorbed. The implication is that as cultural narratives shift — as redemption stories give way to stories of structural victimhood, for example — the ways individuals understand and narrate their own lives shift correspondingly.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: construction of personal identity through culturally shaped self-narratives
psychological benefits of reinterpreting past failures as learning experiences
influence of storytelling traditions on cross-cultural moral reasoning
limitations of using narrative frameworks in clinical psychology
ways that cultural change alters the social functions of traditional myths

explanations.correct: ① 문화적으로 형성된 자아 서사를 통한 개인 정체성 구성을 정확히 포착합니다. 지문은 "individual identity is never entirely individual: it is co-authored by the cultural repertoire of stories a person has absorbed"(개인 정체성은 결코 완전히 개인적이지 않으며, 흡수된 문화적 서사 레퍼토리에 의해 공동 저술된다)고 명시합니다.

wrong.2: ② 과거 실패 재해석의 심리적 이점은 지문의 초점이 아닙니다. 지문은 처방이 아닌 이론적 설명입니다.
wrong.3: ③ 이야기 전통과 문화 간 도덕적 추론의 관계는 다루지 않습니다. 지문은 개인 정체성 형성에 관한 것입니다.
wrong.4: ④ 임상 심리학의 한계는 전혀 언급되지 않습니다.
wrong.5: ⑤ 전통 신화 자체가 분석 대상이 아닙니다. 문화적 서사 템플릿은 개인이 사용하는 도구로 언급됩니다.

explanations.key_point: 인간은 경험을 문화적 서사 템플릿(영웅 여정, 구원 서사 등)에 맞춰 자아 이야기로 구성하므로, 개인 정체성은 문화와 공동 저술된다.

self_check.passage_topic: narrative identity: self-construction via culturally shaped life stories
self_check.main_argument: Personal identity is built through a selective life narrative that draws on cultural story templates, meaning individual identity is always partly culturally co-authored.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 1 captures both the personal construction dimension and the cultural shaping dimension that the passage argues are inseparable.

distractor_analysis.choice_2: Reinterpreting past failures is never the focus; the passage is theoretical, not prescriptive about coping strategies.
distractor_analysis.choice_3: Cross-cultural moral reasoning is unrelated; the passage discusses individual identity formation, not ethics across cultures.
distractor_analysis.choice_4: Clinical psychology limitations are never mentioned; the passage is theoretical about identity, not applied to therapy.
distractor_analysis.choice_5: Traditional myths are not the subject; cultural narrative templates are mentioned as tools individuals use, not as objects of analysis themselves.

self_check.difficulty: challenging — requires grasping the abstract intersection of psychology (narrative identity) and cultural theory (co-authorship by cultural repertoire), and choice 3 uses 'storytelling' to tempt misidentification.

questions.passage: Microplastics — particles less than five millimeters in diameter — have been detected in virtually every environment scientists have examined, from the depths of ocean trenches to Arctic ice cores. Their widespread presence results from the accumulation and fragmentation of larger plastic debris over decades. While the long-term ecological consequences of microplastic pollution are still being studied, early evidence is concerning. Marine organisms from zooplankton to fish have been found to ingest microplastics, which can block digestive tracts, reduce feeding, and transfer associated chemical contaminants up the food chain. On land, studies have detected microplastics in agricultural soils, where they alter soil structure and may inhibit microbial activity essential to nutrient cycling. Even human blood and lung tissue now show detectable levels of microplastic particles, raising urgent questions about human health risks. The scale and ubiquity of this contamination have led many researchers to describe microplastic pollution as one of the defining environmental challenges of the twenty-first century.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: reduction of plastic production as a solution to marine pollution
pervasive presence and ecological consequences of microplastic pollution
technological methods for filtering microplastics from drinking water
history of international agreements on plastic waste reduction
chemical composition of different types of plastic debris in oceans

explanations.correct: ② 미세플라스틱 오염의 만연한 존재와 생태적 결과를 정확히 포착합니다. 지문은 "detected in virtually every environment"(거의 모든 환경에서 검출)로 편재성을 확인하고, 해양 생물·농경 토양·인체 혈액까지 영향이 미침을 서술하며 "one of the defining environmental challenges of the twenty-first century"(21세기의 결정적 환경 과제 중 하나)라고 결론짓습니다.

wrong.1: ① 플라스틱 생산 감축은 해결책으로 전혀 언급되지 않습니다. 지문은 설명적입니다.
wrong.3: ③ 식수 필터링 기술은 논의되지 않습니다.
wrong.4: ④ 국제 협약의 역사는 전혀 언급되지 않습니다.
wrong.5: ⑤ 플라스틱 잔해의 화학적 조성은 주제가 아닙니다. 지문은 환경 내 존재와 영향을 다룹니다.

explanations.key_point: 미세플라스틱은 해양·토양·인체 등 거의 모든 환경에 편재하며 생태계와 건강에 심각한 위협을 가하는 21세기의 환경 과제이다.

self_check.passage_topic: microplastic pollution: ubiquity and ecological/health impacts
self_check.main_argument: Microplastics are found everywhere and pose documented risks to marine life, soil ecosystems, and potentially human health, making them a defining environmental challenge.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 2 captures both the main descriptive claim (pervasive presence) and the argumentative claim (ecological consequences) of the passage.

distractor_analysis.choice_1: Plastic production reduction is never mentioned as a solution; the passage is explanatory, not prescriptive.
distractor_analysis.choice_3: Water filtration technology is not discussed.
distractor_analysis.choice_4: International agreements are never mentioned.
distractor_analysis.choice_5: Chemical composition is not the focus; the passage discusses the environmental presence and effects, not the chemistry of plastic types.

self_check.difficulty: easy — the passage has a clear two-part structure (ubiquity + consequences) that the correct answer directly reflects.

questions.passage: In the history of Western music, silence has often been treated as merely the absence of sound — a neutral backdrop against which melodies and harmonies occur. John Cage's famous composition 4'33", in which a performer sits at a piano without playing a single note for four minutes and thirty-three seconds, fundamentally challenged this assumption. Cage argued that silence is not emptiness but an invitation to listen: during a performance, audiences discover that the concert hall is never truly quiet. Coughs, rustling programs, traffic noise, and the sounds of breathing all become the composition. What 4'33" demonstrated is that music is not defined by sound alone but by the act of attentive listening itself. By directing audience attention toward ambient sound, Cage dissolved the conventional boundary between musical and non-musical sound, arguing that everything we listen to with intention can be music. His work remains a touchstone for debates about the definition of music and the role of the listener as co-creator.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: historical overview of experimental music movements in the twentieth century
Cage's redefinition of music and silence through attentive listening
psychological effects of silence on concentration and creative thinking
influence of ambient noise on the emotional experience of concert audiences
role of performance venues in shaping how music is perceived

explanations.correct: ② 케이지의 음악과 침묵에 대한 재정의를 정확히 포착합니다. 지문은 4'33"이 "music is not defined by sound alone but by the act of attentive listening itself"(음악은 소리만으로 정의되지 않고 주의 깊은 듣기 행위 자체로 정의된다)를 보여주었다고 명시하며, 케이지가 "dissolved the conventional boundary between musical and non-musical sound"(음악적·비음악적 소리의 경계를 허물었다)고 설명합니다.

questions.passage: For centuries, maps were understood as objective representations of geographic reality — faithful documents that reduced the physical world to a manageable image. Critical cartographers have dismantled this assumption. Every map involves choices: what to include and what to omit, what scale to use, which projection to adopt, what names to assign to places. These choices are never neutral. The Mercator projection, designed for maritime navigation in 1569, distorts the size of landmasses so significantly that Greenland appears larger than Africa, even though Africa is fourteen times bigger. This distortion persisted on classroom walls for centuries, subtly shaping students' spatial perceptions of the world and its regions. Place names, too, encode power: the same body of water has historically been called the Sea of Japan by some and the East Sea by others, and the choice of name reflects political claims and historical grievances. Maps, in short, are rhetorical documents that construct as much as they describe, embedding the perspectives and interests of their makers into what appears to be objective geographic fact.

questions.passage: Urban heat islands form when natural land cover is replaced by surfaces such as asphalt, concrete, and rooftops that absorb and retain solar heat more efficiently than vegetation. Cities can be several degrees warmer than surrounding rural areas, and this temperature differential widens at night when urban surfaces slowly release accumulated heat. The ecological and public health consequences are significant. Higher temperatures increase energy demand for cooling, which in turn increases greenhouse gas emissions, creating a feedback loop that further warms both the city and the global climate. Heat-related illness and mortality rise disproportionately among urban poor communities with limited access to air conditioning. Researchers have found that strategic interventions — increasing tree canopy coverage, installing green roofs, and replacing dark pavements with reflective materials — can substantially reduce urban temperatures. Importantly, these solutions are not purely technical: their equitable distribution across neighborhoods requires deliberate policy decisions that prioritize communities with the greatest heat exposure and fewest adaptive resources.

questions.passage: In neoclassical economics, prices are treated as signals that aggregate and transmit information dispersed across millions of economic actors. When demand for a commodity rises, its price increases, signaling producers to expand supply and consumers to economize — all without any central coordinator. Friedrich Hayek argued that this price mechanism accomplishes something no central planning authority ever could: it processes knowledge that is inherently local, tacit, and personal — knowledge about particular circumstances of time and place that no single mind or institution could gather and process. However, digital platforms have introduced a new and complicating dynamic. Firms like ride-sharing companies and e-commerce platforms possess asymmetric information advantages: through their apps, they observe demand patterns, user behaviors, and supply conditions in real time and at scale, while individual participants remain largely in the dark. These platforms do not merely respond to prices — they set and adjust them algorithmically, in ways that users cannot observe or contest. The question this raises is whether platform pricing represents a sophisticated extension of the price mechanism Hayek celebrated or, rather, a form of private central planning that undermines the epistemic virtues he attributed to decentralized markets.

choices: advantages of decentralized markets over government-controlled economic systems

questions.passage: Volunteering and community service have long been promoted primarily for their social benefits — the ways they help recipients and strengthen communities. A growing body of psychological research, however, reveals equally significant benefits for the volunteers themselves. Studies consistently find that people who regularly volunteer report higher levels of life satisfaction, lower rates of depression, and greater sense of purpose compared to non-volunteers. The mechanism appears to involve both social connection and what psychologists call "helper's high" — a mood-enhancing neurological response associated with acts of giving. Older adults who volunteer show slower cognitive decline and lower rates of early mortality than matched non-volunteers, suggesting that prosocial behavior may have direct physiological benefits. Importantly, these benefits are maximized when volunteering is motivated by genuine concern for others rather than external rewards or social obligations. When people volunteer primarily to fulfill requirements or gain recognition, the psychological benefits are substantially reduced, suggesting that intrinsic motivation is a key moderating variable.

questions.passage:
Coral reefs cover less than one percent of the ocean floor yet support an estimated twenty-five percent of all marine species. This disproportionate ecological productivity has puzzled scientists, given that coral reefs typically grow in nutrient-poor tropical waters — a paradox sometimes called the "Darwin Paradox" after the naturalist who first noted it. The answer lies in the extraordinarily efficient recycling of nutrients within reef ecosystems. Symbiotic algae called zooxanthellae live within coral tissues and provide corals with up to ninety percent of their energy through photosynthesis, while corals in turn supply the algae with nutrients from their waste products. This tight mutualism creates a nearly closed nutrient cycle that minimizes loss to the surrounding water. Additionally, the complex physical structure of reefs creates thousands of microhabitats, enabling co-existence of species with highly specialized ecological roles. Together, these mechanisms explain how reefs sustain remarkable biodiversity in waters that would otherwise support very little life.
* mutualism: 상리공생

questions.passage: The dominant model in management theory for much of the twentieth century treated workers as rational economic agents whose primary motivation was financial compensation. If you paid people enough, they would work hard; if they underperformed, you adjusted their incentives. This view, derived partly from Frederick Taylor's scientific management principles, reduced motivation to a simple input-output equation. Subsequent research in organizational psychology has progressively undermined this model. Edward Deci and Richard Ryan's self-determination theory proposes that human motivation is fundamentally driven by three innate psychological needs: autonomy (the desire to direct one's own actions), competence (the desire to develop mastery), and relatedness (the desire for meaningful social connection). When work environments satisfy these needs, employees exhibit intrinsic motivation — they pursue tasks because the work itself is meaningful, not merely because of external rewards. Critically, research shows that under certain conditions, introducing external rewards for intrinsically motivated activities can actually reduce motivation — a phenomenon termed the "overjustification effect."

choices: importance of competitive wages in reducing employee turnover rates

role of workplace relationships in preventing professional burnout
advantages of flexible work arrangements for enhancing job satisfaction

questions.passage: The rise of streaming platforms has fundamentally altered the economics of music consumption. Prior to digital streaming, record labels derived revenue primarily from the sale of physical copies — albums and singles — meaning that consumer ownership of music was the economic backbone of the industry. Streaming replaced ownership with access: for a monthly subscription fee, or for free with advertising, listeners can access virtually the entire history of recorded music on demand. While this arrangement has dramatically increased the volume of music consumption and expanded audiences globally, it has also compressed per-stream royalty rates to fractions of a cent. Independent artists and mid-tier musicians — those without the scale to accumulate hundreds of millions of streams — find it increasingly difficult to generate sustainable income from recorded music alone. As a result, the industry has seen a strategic reorientation: live performance, merchandise, synchronization licensing, and direct fan support platforms have become more economically central, particularly for artists outside the commercial mainstream. Recording, once the industry's core product, has become in many cases a promotional vehicle for these other revenue sources.

questions.passage: In philosophy of mind, the "extended mind" thesis, proposed by Andy Clark and David Chalmers, challenges the assumption that cognition is confined to the brain and skull. Clark and Chalmers argue that when an external tool or environment plays a functional role equivalent to an internal cognitive process, that tool or environment should be considered part of the cognitive system itself. A person with Alzheimer's disease who relies on a notebook to store information that their biological memory can no longer retain is, on this view, engaging in a genuinely cognitive process — not merely using a cognitive prosthetic. The notebook functions as memory. Critics argue that this extension of mind is too permissive: it would seem to imply that any calculator, smartphone, or search engine constitutes part of our mind whenever we use it. Clark and Chalmers respond that the relevant criterion is not physical location but functional integration — the degree to which an external resource is seamlessly incorporated into the agent's cognitive economy. The debate has significant implications for how we conceptualize intelligence, responsibility, and cognitive enhancement.

questions.passage: Physical exercise has long been associated with improvements in mood and mental health, but researchers have increasingly sought to understand the specific mechanisms behind these effects. One well-established pathway involves the release of endorphins, naturally occurring chemicals that reduce pain perception and produce feelings of euphoria. The so-called "runner's high" is a commonly reported example. However, more recent research suggests that endorphins may not be the whole story. Studies using drugs that block endorphin receptors find that exercise still improves mood, pointing to other mechanisms. One leading candidate is brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF), a protein that promotes the growth and maintenance of neurons and is released during aerobic activity. Elevated BDNF levels have been associated with improved mood, reduced anxiety, and enhanced cognitive function. Exercise also promotes neurogenesis — the growth of new neurons — in the hippocampus, a brain region critical to memory and emotional regulation. Together, these findings suggest that exercise benefits mental health through multiple biological pathways, not merely the endorphin system.

choices: types of physical exercise most effective for treating clinical depression

questions.passage: Traditionally, the relationship between science and technology was understood as unidirectional: science produced knowledge, and technology applied that knowledge to practical problems. This sequencing — basic research first, application second — was institutionalized in postwar science policy, most influentially through Vannevar Bush's 1945 report Science, the Endless Frontier, which argued that government investment in basic research would eventually yield technological and economic dividends. However, historians and philosophers of science have increasingly argued that this linear model distorts the actual relationship. Many important scientific breakthroughs emerged not from the pure pursuit of abstract knowledge but from the process of trying to make technologies work. The development of thermodynamics as a scientific discipline, for example, arose largely from practical efforts to improve the efficiency of steam engines, not the other way around. Similarly, the invention of the transistor depended on materials science and quantum mechanical insights that were developed partly in response to engineering needs. Technology, on this view, is not merely the application of science — it is also one of science's most productive laboratories.

choices: economic arguments for increasing government funding of basic science research

challenge to the linear model of science preceding and enabling technology
importance of interdisciplinary collaboration for scientific breakthroughs

questions.passage: The practice of solitary confinement — isolating prisoners in individual cells for twenty-two to twenty-four hours a day — has been defended on grounds of institutional safety, as a means of managing the most dangerous or disruptive inmates. However, a large body of research in psychiatry and neuroscience has concluded that prolonged isolation causes severe and often irreversible psychological harm. Studies document that after as few as a few days of isolation, individuals begin to experience perceptual distortions, anxiety, hallucinations, and cognitive decline. The human nervous system, shaped by evolutionary pressures toward social existence, appears fundamentally ill-equipped to tolerate the absence of meaningful social interaction. Critics argue that solitary confinement therefore constitutes a form of torture, regardless of its institutional justification, and that its use violates international human rights standards. Several countries and some states in the United States have moved to restrict or eliminate solitary confinement, replacing it with therapeutic and rehabilitative approaches to managing difficult inmates.

questions.passage: When consumers encounter a product associated with a celebrity, their evaluation of that product is often influenced not by any logical connection between the celebrity and the product but by a process psychologists call evaluative conditioning. Simply by being repeatedly paired with a liked or admired person, a product can acquire some of that person's positive associations. The phenomenon helps explain why brands pay millions to have athletes endorse running shoes, actors appear in perfume advertisements, or musicians partner with beverage companies — even when those celebrities have no particular expertise relevant to the product. Research has also shown that the effectiveness of celebrity endorsement is moderated by the concept of "match-up" — the perceived fit between the celebrity's image and the product category. A rugged outdoor adventurer endorsing energy drinks creates a stronger positive transfer of associations than endorsing luxury watches, because the fit is more intuitively coherent. Conversely, a mismatch can actually harm brand perception if consumers notice and react to the incongruence between endorser identity and product values.

questions.passage:
Soil is commonly understood as an inert substrate — a passive medium in which plants grow and upon which buildings stand. This view, while intuitive, fundamentally misrepresents what soil actually is: a living ecosystem of extraordinary complexity. A single teaspoon of healthy agricultural soil can contain more microorganisms than there are people on Earth. These organisms — bacteria, fungi, archaea, nematodes, protozoa, and countless others — are not mere bystanders but active engineers of soil chemistry, structure, and fertility. Mycorrhizal fungi, for instance, extend their networks through soil to connect the root systems of multiple plants, transferring carbon, water, and nutrients between hosts in exchanges that can benefit or disadvantage individual plants depending on ecological context. The health of this subterranean microbiome directly determines the fertility and carbon-sequestration capacity of soil. Industrial agriculture's reliance on synthetic fertilizers and pesticides has been shown to dramatically reduce soil microbial diversity — a degradation that may not be immediately visible in crop yields but undermines the long-term resilience of agricultural systems in ways that current productivity metrics fail to capture.
* archaea: 고세균

questions.passage: When people make decisions under uncertainty, they consistently display a preference for losses avoided over equivalent gains obtained — a pattern psychologists call loss aversion. In a classic demonstration, people who are offered a gamble where they could either gain fifty dollars or lose fifty dollars with equal probability will typically refuse the gamble, even though its expected monetary value is zero. To accept the gamble, most people require the potential gain to be roughly twice the potential loss. This asymmetry between the weight given to losses and gains pervades human decision making across contexts from investment behavior and health choices to negotiations and consumer decisions. Loss aversion is particularly pronounced when people feel they already own or are entitled to something: the pain of losing what one has is felt more acutely than the pleasure of gaining something of equivalent value. Marketers routinely exploit this bias by framing promotions as avoiding a loss rather than obtaining a gain — "Don't miss out" is psychologically more compelling than "Get more."
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: strategies for making rational financial decisions in uncertain market conditions
cognitive biases that lead to overconfidence in high-stakes decision making
cultural differences in attitudes toward financial risk and investment behavior
influence of expected value calculations on consumer purchasing decisions
loss aversion as a pervasive bias affecting human decision making

explanations.correct: ⑤ 인간 의사 결정에 만연한 손실 회피 편향을 정확히 포착합니다. 지문은 "people consistently display a preference for losses avoided over equivalent gains obtained"(사람들은 일관되게 동등한 이득보다 손실 회피를 선호한다)는 심리를 소개하고, "This asymmetry between the weight given to losses and gains pervades human decision making across contexts"(손실과 이득에 부여되는 가중치의 비대칭이 모든 맥락의 인간 의사 결정에 만연해 있다)고 명시합니다.

wrong.1: ① 합리적 금융 결정 전략은 논의되지 않습니다. 지문은 설명적·분석적입니다.
wrong.2: ② 과신 편향은 전혀 언급되지 않습니다. 지문은 손실 회피에 특화되어 있습니다.
wrong.3: ③ 문화적 차이는 언급되지 않습니다. 손실 회피는 보편적 경향으로 제시됩니다.
wrong.4: ④ 기대값 계산은 손실 회피 시연을 위한 설정 장치로만 사용됩니다.

explanations.key_point: 손실 회피는 손실이 동등한 이득보다 약 두 배 크게 느껴지는 보편적 심리 편향으로, 투자·소비자 결정부터 마케팅 프레이밍까지 광범위하게 작용한다.

self_check.passage_topic: loss aversion bias in human decision making
self_check.main_argument: Loss aversion is a robust cognitive bias where losses are weighted more heavily than equivalent gains, influencing decisions across contexts from finance to consumer behavior.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 5 directly and precisely names the bias (loss aversion) and its defining characteristic (pervasive influence on human decision making) as argued throughout the passage.

distractor_analysis.choice_1: Rational financial decision strategies are not discussed; the passage is descriptive and explanatory about the bias.
distractor_analysis.choice_2: Overconfidence bias is not discussed; the passage is specifically about loss aversion.
distractor_analysis.choice_3: Cultural differences are not mentioned; loss aversion is presented as a universal human tendency.

questions.passage: The emergence of citizen journalism — the practice of ordinary individuals reporting and sharing news through social media and independent blogs — has been celebrated as a democratization of information. Where once only credentialed reporters with institutional backing could distribute news to mass audiences, today any smartphone user can broadcast events in real time to millions. This has undeniable advantages: events that might have gone unreported are now documented and shared, and powerful institutions face greater accountability when their actions are immediately visible. However, the absence of editorial standards, fact-checking processes, and professional accountability norms that characterize institutional journalism creates significant vulnerabilities. Misinformation, once shared, spreads through social networks far faster than corrections, and emotionally charged content consistently outperforms accurate but less engaging information in algorithms that prioritize engagement. The democratization of news production has thus produced a paradox: while the sources of information have multiplied and diversified, the reliability of the information environment has deteriorated, and distinguishing credible reporting from misinformation has become more rather than less difficult.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: importance of media literacy education in the digital age
legal protections available to citizen journalists under press freedom laws
role of social media algorithms in determining political opinion formation
strategies used by professional journalists to verify online sources
paradox of citizen journalism: expanded sources but reduced information reliability

explanations.correct: ⑤ 시민 저널리즘의 역설—소스 확대와 정보 신뢰도 저하—을 정확히 포착합니다. 지문은 "the sources of information have multiplied and diversified"(정보 출처가 증가·다양화되었지만) "the reliability of the information environment has deteriorated"(정보 환경의 신뢰도는 저하되었으며), "distinguishing credible reporting from misinformation has become more rather than less difficult"(신뢰할 수 있는 보도와 허위 정보를 구별하기가 더 어려워졌다)는 역설적 결론을 제시합니다.

wrong.1: ① 미디어 리터러시 교육은 해결책으로 제시되지 않습니다.
wrong.2: ② 시민 기자의 법적 보호는 전혀 언급되지 않습니다.
wrong.3: ③ 알고리즘이 정치적 여론 형성에 미치는 영향은 주제가 아닙니다. 알고리즘은 허위 정보 확산 이유로만 언급됩니다.
wrong.4: ④ 전문 기자의 검증 전략은 논의되지 않습니다.

self_check.passage_topic: citizen journalism paradox: democratization of news alongside reduced reliability
self_check.main_argument: Citizen journalism has democratized news production but created a paradox: more sources with lower reliability, making it harder to distinguish credible reporting from misinformation.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 5 captures the passage's core paradox: the simultaneous expansion of sources and deterioration of reliability.

questions.passage: The concept of "nudging," popularized by behavioral economists Richard Thaler and Cass Sunstein, proposes that individuals can be guided toward better decisions by restructuring the choice environment without restricting options or changing incentives. Classic nudges include making organ donation the default option rather than requiring active enrollment, placing healthy foods at eye level in cafeterias, or presenting energy consumption data alongside comparisons to neighbors' usage. Proponents argue that nudges are a superior policy tool because they preserve individual freedom while leveraging well-documented cognitive biases — such as default effect, social norms, and loss aversion — to steer people toward outcomes that are better for them and society. Critics, however, raise concerns about the paternalistic assumptions embedded in nudge design: who decides what outcomes are "better," and is it ethical to exploit cognitive biases even in service of beneficial goals? Moreover, nudges may be less effective when people become aware of them, and their cumulative effects may be to habituate citizens to decision environments designed by others rather than to develop genuine autonomous decision-making capacity.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: economic principles behind designing effective public health campaigns
comparison of nudges and financial incentives as behavior change tools
role of behavioral economics in reforming national tax collection systems
ways that default settings in digital platforms manipulate consumer behavior
ethical and practical debate over the use of nudges as a policy tool

explanations.correct: ⑤ 넛지를 정책 도구로 활용하는 것에 대한 윤리적·실용적 논쟁을 정확히 포착합니다. 지문은 넛지가 개인의 자유를 보존하면서 인지 편향을 활용한다는 옹호론과, "who decides what outcomes are 'better'"(누가 더 나은 결과를 결정하는가)라는 온정주의적 전제에 대한 비판 및 누적 효과로 "genuine autonomous decision-making capacity"(진정한 자율적 의사 결정 능력)가 위축될 수 있다는 실용적 우려를 모두 다룹니다.

wrong.1: ① 공중 보건 캠페인 설계의 경제적 원리는 주제가 아닙니다.
wrong.2: ② 넛지와 금전적 인센티브의 명시적 비교는 이루어지지 않습니다.
wrong.3: ③ 세금 징수 개혁은 언급되지 않습니다.
wrong.4: ④ 디지털 플랫폼은 논의되지 않습니다. 지문의 예시는 구내식당·장기 기증 양식 등 물리적 환경입니다.

explanations.key_point: 넛지는 자유를 보존하면서 행동을 유도하는 정책 도구로 각광받지만, 온정주의적 가정과 자율적 의사 결정 능력 위축이라는 윤리적·실용적 논쟁에 직면해 있다.

self_check.passage_topic: nudge theory: benefits and ethical/practical criticisms
self_check.main_argument: Nudges are celebrated as freedom-preserving policy tools but face substantive ethical critiques about paternalism and practical concerns about their long-term effects on autonomous decision-making.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 5 captures both dimensions the passage develops: the debate is both ethical (paternalism) and practical (effectiveness, habituation).

questions.passage:
The concept of "epistemic injustice," introduced by philosopher Miranda Fricker, identifies a form of wrongdoing that occurs specifically in the domain of knowledge and inquiry. Fricker distinguishes two main varieties. Testimonial injustice occurs when a speaker's testimony is given less credibility than it deserves due to prejudice about their identity — as when a woman's account of workplace discrimination is disbelieved because of gender bias, or when a patient's self-report of symptoms is dismissed by a physician due to racial assumptions about pain tolerance. Hermeneutical injustice occurs when a gap in collective interpretive resources leaves someone unable to make sense of their own significant social experience — as occurred with sexual harassment before that concept was available to name and organize a previously nameless phenomenon. What makes epistemic injustice distinct from other forms of injustice is that its harm is primary, not merely instrumental: the wrong is done to someone specifically in their capacity as a knower — as someone who generates, shares, and makes sense of knowledge. This identification has proven generative for feminist philosophy, critical race theory, and medical ethics alike.
* hermeneutical: 해석학적
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: philosophical critique of objectivity standards in scientific research
role of language barriers in creating misunderstandings between cultures
impact of social media on the spread of misinformation in public discourse
challenges of achieving legal equality for historically marginalized groups
types and nature of epistemic injustice as wrongdoing in the domain of knowledge

explanations.correct: ⑤ 인식론적 불의의 유형과 본질을 정확히 포착합니다. 지문은 프리커의 두 가지 유형인 증언적 불의("a speaker's testimony is given less credibility... due to prejudice about their identity", 정체성에 대한 편견으로 발화자의 증언 신뢰도가 낮게 평가됨)와 해석학적 불의("a gap in collective interpretive resources leaves someone unable to make sense of their own significant social experience", 집단적 해석 자원의 공백으로 개인이 자신의 중요한 사회적 경험을 이해하지 못함)를 구분하고, 이것이 앎의 주체로서 입는 일차적 해악임을 설명합니다.

wrong.1: ① 과학 연구의 객관성 기준에 대한 철학적 비판은 논의되지 않습니다.
wrong.2: ② 언어 장벽과 문화 간 오해는 주제가 아닙니다.
wrong.3: ③ 소셜 미디어와 허위 정보는 전혀 언급되지 않습니다.
wrong.4: ④ 법적 평등 달성의 어려움은 주제가 아닙니다. 인식론적 불의는 법적 지위가 아닌 지식 생산 영역에 관한 것입니다.

explanations.key_point: 인식론적 불의는 증언적 불의(신뢰도 부정)와 해석학적 불의(해석 자원 결여)라는 두 가지 형태로, 개인을 앎의 주체로서 부당하게 해친다.

self_check.passage_topic: Miranda Fricker's epistemic injustice: testimonial and hermeneutical varieties
self_check.main_argument: Epistemic injustice is a form of harm done to persons specifically in their capacity as knowers; it takes two main forms — testimonial (credibility denial) and hermeneutical (interpretive gap) — and is primary, not merely instrumental.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 5 captures both the core concept ('epistemic injustice') and the passage's explanatory goal ('types and nature') of defining and distinguishing this philosophical concept.

distractor_analysis.choice_1: Scientific research objectivity is not discussed; the examples concern social testimony and lived experience.
distractor_analysis.choice_2: Language barriers and cross-cultural misunderstanding are not the subject; the passage is about identity-based credibility deficits.
distractor_analysis.choice_3: Social media and misinformation are not mentioned.
distractor_analysis.choice_4: Legal equality is not the subject; epistemic injustice is a philosophical concept about knowledge-production, not legal status.

self_check.difficulty: challenging — requires understanding a technical philosophical concept (epistemic injustice, hermeneutical) and its two subtypes; choices 1 and 4 are designed to trap students who reduce the passage to a general social justice argument.

questions.passage: Ecological succession describes the predictable sequence of changes that occur in biological communities over time following a disturbance. In primary succession — which occurs on bare substrate where no soil or life previously existed, such as volcanic rock or glacial moraines — pioneer species such as lichens and mosses gradually break down rock, accumulate organic material, and alter microclimatic conditions in ways that make the environment progressively more hospitable to other organisms. As soil develops and conditions change, these pioneers are displaced by subsequent communities of grasses, shrubs, and eventually trees in a process that can span centuries. Secondary succession, which occurs on previously vegetated land after disturbances such as fires or agriculture, is faster because soil and seed banks remain. The endpoint of succession — a stable, self-perpetuating climax community — was long held to be the inevitable goal toward which all successional sequences converge. Contemporary ecology has largely abandoned this teleological view: disturbances are too frequent and unpredictable, and climax communities too variable, for a single stable endpoint to be a universal property of ecosystems. Succession is now understood as contingent, historically dependent, and in many systems, never truly complete.
questions.question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: role of human disturbances in accelerating primary succession processes
differences in biodiversity between pioneer and climax stage communities
impact of volcanic eruptions on long-term soil formation and plant diversity
use of succession models to predict vegetation recovery after wildfires
ecological succession and the revision of the climax community concept

explanations.correct: ⑤ 생태 천이와 극상 군집 개념의 수정을 모두 포착합니다. 지문은 1차·2차 천이를 설명한 후, 극상 군집이라는 안정적 종착점 개념이 현대 생태학에서 "largely abandoned"(대부분 포기되었으며), "Succession is now understood as contingent, historically dependent, and in many systems, never truly complete"(천이는 이제 우연적·역사 의존적이며 결코 완전히 완성되지 않는 것으로 이해된다)고 비판적으로 재평가합니다.

wrong.1: ① 인간 교란이 1차 천이를 가속화하는 것은 논의되지 않습니다.
wrong.2: ② 선구 집단과 극상 집단 간 생물 다양성 비교는 이루어지지 않습니다.

explanations.key_point: 생태 천이는 교란 후 예측 가능한 군집 변화를 서술하지만, 극상 군집이라는 목적론적 종착점 개념은 현대 생태학에서 폐기되었다.

self_check.passage_topic: ecological succession and critique/revision of the climax community concept
self_check.main_argument: Ecological succession describes predictable community change after disturbances; the traditional climax community endpoint has been abandoned in contemporary ecology as too teleological given the contingency and variability of real systems.
self_check.correct_choice_justification: Choice 5 captures both the descriptive content (succession) and the analytical move that distinguishes this passage (the revision/critique of the climax community concept).

distractor_analysis.choice_1: Human disturbances accelerating primary succession are not discussed; primary succession is described in natural contexts.
distractor_analysis.choice_2: Biodiversity comparison between pioneer and climax communities is not made; the passage is about the process and theory of succession.
